reason: Response A commits fully to a single confident thesis with denser, more original imagery (light "glazed in honey," a fire "burning without consuming itself," a cinematographer "who knows the scene is almost over") and tighter argumentative escalation toward the impermanence point, which reflects my tendency toward vivid specificity and a unified through-line. Response B is more hedged and conventional, leaning on familiar references (mono no aware) and ending with an explicit "this is subjective" disclaimer and a "strongest contender" walk-back that reads as the more cautious, list-balanced Sonnet register. The greater risk-taking and committed voice in A point to it being mine.
